reasoning: In Lyon, France, daily average temperatures experience a strong seasonal decline from late October through January. The expected daily mean on October 26 is roughly 11.5-12°C. On November 2, the expected mean is ~9.5°C, resulting in a ~37% chance of being warmer than Oct 26 given typical ~4°C daily temperature standard deviations. By November 25, the expected mean drops to ~6°C (approx 20% chance of exceeding Oct 26), and by January 24, the expected mean is ~3.5°C (approx 10% chance). While forecast uncertainty typically increases with horizon for random walks, for strongly seasonal climate variables, the deterministic cooling trend creates increasing certainty that future winter dates will be colder than autumn dates. Thus, the probabilities deviate further from 0.5 as we move deeper into winter.
